activities_projects.title: Good Manners Checklist
activities_projects.objective: Identify polite words and phrases used in daily life
steps: Read the story 'Say the Right Thing' carefully.
List 5 polite words or phrases mentioned in the text (e.g., 'Thank you', 'Excuse me').
Write down one situation for each word where it should be used.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students recognize the importance of polite vocabulary.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification and situational matching (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Role-Play: The Polite Student
activities_projects.objective: Practice using the 'right' words in social situations
steps: Work with a partner to act out a small scene from the chapter.
One person plays the character who forgets to be polite, and the other corrects them gently.
Switch roles so both practice saying the 'right thing'.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students gain confidence in using polite language orally.
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation and correct use of polite phrases (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The 'Magic Words' Poster
activities_projects.objective: Create a visual reminder of positive communication
steps: Choose three 'Magic Words' from the story.
Draw a colorful poster featuring these words in large letters.
Decorate the poster with symbols of kindness (like a smile or a handshake).
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students associate polite language with positive emotions.
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and clarity of the message (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Happy vs. Sad Words
activities_projects.objective: Understand how words affect the feelings of others
steps: Draw two columns in your notebook: 'Words that make us smile' and 'Words that hurt'.
Place words from the chapter into the correct column.
Explain to a friend why the 'smile' words are the 'right' things to say.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop empathy through language choice.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct categorization and simple reasoning (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Vocabulary: Synonyms for 'Kind'
activities_projects.objective: Expand vocabulary related to positive behavior
steps: Find the word 'Kind' or 'Polite' in the text.
Use a dictionary to find two other words that mean the same (e.g., 'Gentle', 'Courteous').
Write a simple sentence using one of the new words.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students build their vocabulary of descriptive adjectives.
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of synonyms and sentence construction (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The 'Right' Response
steps: Read these three situations: 1. Someone gives you a gift. 2. You bump into someone. 3. You want to borrow a pen.
Write the 'Right Thing' to say for each situation based on the story.
Share your answers with the class.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students apply social etiquette to common scenarios.
activities_projects.evaluation: Appropriateness of the chosen responses (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Drawing a Story Strip
activities_projects.objective: Summarize a key lesson through a comic strip
steps: Pick one scene from the chapter where a character says the 'wrong' thing and then corrects it.
Draw a 3-panel comic strip: Panel 1 (The mistake), Panel 2 (The realization), Panel 3 (The right thing).
Add simple speech bubbles.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students visualize narrative progression and moral lessons.
activities_projects.evaluation: Storytelling clarity and relevance to the theme (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Opposite Actions
steps: Identify words like 'Polite', 'Helpful', and 'Patient' in the story.
Write down their opposites (e.g., 'Rude', 'Unhelpful').
Write one sentence about why we should choose the positive word.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the contrast between good and bad manners.

activities_projects.title: A 'Thank You' Note
activities_projects.objective: Practice writing a short, polite letter
steps: Think of someone who helped you today.
Write a 2-sentence 'Thank You' note to them.
Use at least one phrase you learned from the chapter 'Say the Right Thing'.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students practice formalizing gratitude through writing.

activities_projects.title: The Echo Game
activities_projects.objective: Improve listening and repetition of polite phrases
steps: The teacher says a polite phrase from the book with a specific emotion (e.g., a happy 'Good morning!').
The students repeat it exactly with the same tone.
Discuss how the *way* we say things is just as important as the words themselves.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the importance of tone and intonation.
